### Post by DarkAz on 2008-04-28
First of all hello and good morning/afternoon/evening.

I'm currently stuck at trying to get my Desktop to clone to my TV.

This is my xorg.conf
```

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Device0"
	Boardname	"GeForce 7300 GS"
	Busid		"PCI:2:0:0"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Option 		"RenderAccel" "true"
	Screen	0
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
	Vendorname	"MAG Technology Co., Ltd."
	Modelname	"MAG 786FD"
	Horizsync	30.0-86.0
	Vertrefresh	50.0-160.0
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@72" 31.5 640 664 704 832 480 489 491 520 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@75" 31.5 640 656 720 840 480 481 484 500 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@85" 36.0 640 696 752 832 480 481 484 509 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@72" 50.0 800 856 976 1040 600 637 643 666 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@75" 49.5 800 816 896 1056 600 601 604 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@85" 56.3 800 832 896 1048 600 601 604 631 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@85" 94.5 1024 1072 1168 1376 768 769 772 808 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@43" 44.9 1024 1032 1208 1264 768 768 776 817 +hsync interlace +vsync
  modeline  "1280x1024@75" 135.0 1280 1296 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Device0"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Option 		"TwinView"
	Option 		"RenderAccel" "true"
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
Modes	"1280x1024@60"	"1280x960@75"	"1280x960@85"	"1400x1050@60"	"1280x960@60"	"1400x1050@75"	"1280x1024@75"	"1600x1200@65"	"1152x864@75"	"1600x1200@60"	"1024x768@43"	"1792x1344@60"	"1024x768@60"	"1856x1392@60"	"1024x768@70"	"1024x768@75"	"1024x768@85"	"832x624@75"	"800x600@60"	"800x600@85"	"800x600@75"	"800x600@72"	"800x600@56"	"640x480@85"	"640x480@75"	"640x480@72"	"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
EndSection
Section "device" # 
	Identifier	"device1"
	Boardname	"GeForce 7300 GS"
	Busid		"PCI:2:0:0"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Screen	1
EndSection
Section "screen" # 
	Identifier	"screen1"
	Device		"device1"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Monitor		"monitor1"
EndSection
Section "monitor" # 
	Identifier	"monitor1"
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

```

PC screen works fine at 1240x1024. However there's no cloning. If I add for example this line:
```

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 	"Default Screen"
**  screen 1      "screen1" clone "screen0"**
EndSection
```

The cloning works but the screen gets stuck at 800x600@100 or 640x480@101. To top it off with this change MPlayer only works with the x11 video driver.

I tried adding a line to use a 800x600 resolution for the TV but no go. I pretty sure there's something wrong in the xorg.conf, but I can't find it. Thanks in advance to all who reply.

### Post by moffa on 2008-04-28
Have you tried using 'nvidia-settings'?

It has a nice GUI configuration tool.
